diagrams.title: Transmission Media Classification Overview
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing the two main categories of transmission media: Guided (bounded) and Unguided (wireless/unbounded), with their sub-categories. This helps students understand the fundamental classification in computer networks.
elements: Two main branch nodes: 'Guided Media' and 'Unguided Media'
Guided Media sub-nodes: Twisted Pair Cable, Coaxial Cable, Fiber Optic Cable
Unguided Media sub-nodes: Radio Waves, Microwaves, Infrared, Satellite Communication
diagrams.purpose: To provide a clear visual overview of how transmission media are categorized in computer networks, establishing the framework for deeper learning.

diagrams.title: Guided Transmission Media Types
diagrams.description: A comparative visualization of three guided/bounded transmission media: Twisted Pair Cable (with UTP/STP variants), Coaxial Cable (with layers visible), and Fiber Optic Cable (showing core, cladding, and jacket). Shows signal confinement within physical pathways.
elements: Three horizontal cable representations with cross-section views
Twisted Pair: Shows twisted wire pairs with optional shielding, labels for UTP/STP
Coaxial Cable: Shows central copper conductor, dielectric insulation, braided shield, outer jacket
Fiber Optic: Shows core, cladding, and coating layers with light ray indication
Signal flow arrows inside each cable type
Key characteristics labels (bandwidth, distance, use cases)
diagrams.purpose: To visually demonstrate how guided media physically confine and direct signals through physical cables, helping students understand bounded transmission.

diagrams.title: Unguided (Wireless) Transmission Media
diagrams.description: An illustration showing various unguided/wireless transmission methods propagating through space without physical cables. Includes radio waves, microwaves, infrared, and satellite communication with their propagation patterns.
elements: Four quadrant layout showing different wireless transmission types
Radio Waves: Antenna towers broadcasting in all directions, labeled frequency range
Microwaves: Point-to-point directional beams between two antennas
Infrared: Line-of-sight propagation from remote control/device
Satellite Communication: Earth stations communicating via space satellite with uplink/downlink beams
Signal wave patterns for each type
diagrams.purpose: To explain how unguided media transmit signals through the atmosphere or space without physical conductors, demonstrating the concept of unbounded/wireless communication.
